title: pelargonium quercinum
intro: Pelargonium quercinum, also known as the Cape Oak-leaved Geranium, is a species of flowering plant in the Geraniaceae family, native to South Africa. Traditionally, the leaves of Pelargonium quercinum have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to provide relief from digestive problems, coughs, and colds, and it has been used to soothe sore throats and reduce fever. Pelargonium quercinum is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in various herbal preparations, including capsules and syrups.
